title: Cynanchum clausum
taxonomy: Cynanchum clausum belongs to the Apocynaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. This family is further divided into various subfamilies and tribes, with Cynanchum clausum being a part of the Asclepiadoideae subfamily. Within this subfamily, Cynanchum clausum is classified as a member of the Asclepiadeae tribe. The classification of Cynanchum clausum reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Apocynaceae family. The specific classification of Cynanchum clausum is based on morphological, anatomical, and molecular characteristics.
uses: Cynanchum clausum has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
